basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: • Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/UniFi%20Site%20Manager%20%28CCF%29/ReleaseNotes.md) • There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The [UniFi Site Manager](https://unifi.ui.com/) solution for Microsoft Sentinel provides cloud-side telemetry ingestion via the [Site Manager API](https://developer.ui.com/site-manager-api/) for sites, devices, hosts and ISP metrics. Ships analytics rules covering ISP downtime, WAN issues, IPS/IDS posture, firmware drift, device offline events, configuration changes and security signals, plus an operations workbook for at-a-glance estate health. **Data Connector:** UniFi Site Manager (CCF) — single Connect deploys 4 polling rules with a single API key. **Underlying API tier:** the Site Manager API is available on all UniFi cloud plans. The Audit log endpoint requires Pro+; this solution does not depend on it. **Pre-requisites:** A UniFi Site Manager API key is required. Generate one at https://unifi.ui.com/api. **Data Connectors:** 1, **Workbooks:** 1, **Analytic Rules:** 21, **Hunting Queries:** 8 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

basics.toolTip: This filters by workspaces that exist in the Resource Group selected

basics.name: workspace
basics.label: Workspace

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for UniFi Site Manager (CCF). You can get UniFi Site Manager (CCF) data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: This solution installs workbook(s) to help you gain insights into the telemetry collected in Microsoft Sentinel. After installing the solution, start using the workbook in Manage solution view.

elements.name: workbook1
elements.label: UniFi Site Manager

options.text: Operational and security telemetry for UniFi cloud-managed sites: site health, device inventory, ISP performance, multi-WAN failover, security posture, and open Sentinel incidents.
This solution installs the following analytic rule templates. After installing the solution, create and enable analytic rules in Manage solution view.

elements.name: analytic1
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Controller Connection State Change

options.text: Detects when a UniFi controller (Cloud Key, Dream Machine, etc.) experiences a connection state change. This could indicate network issues, power problems, or controller failures that may impact netwo

elements.name: analytic2
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Data Connector Health

options.text: Monitors the health of UniFi Site Manager data connectors and alerts when data ingestion stops or becomes stale. This ensures visibility into your UniFi infrastructure is maintained.

elements.name: analytic3
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Device Offline

options.text: Detects when a UniFi device goes offline. This could indicate network issues, power problems, or device failure requiring immediate attention.

elements.name: analytic4
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: External WAN IP changed

options.text: A site reported more than one distinct WAN external IP within an hour. Could be ISP DHCP refresh, a WAN reconfiguration, or in the worst case a routing hijack. Verify against expected ISP behaviour.

elements.name: analytic5
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Firmware Update Available

options.text: Detects when UniFi devices have firmware updates available. Keeping firmware updated is critical for security as updates often contain patches for vulnerabilities.

elements.name: analytic6
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: IPS/IDS disabled or misconfigured

options.text: Gateway threat-protection state TRANSITIONED away from the expected IPS-active mode. Fires only on the change (was protected, now degraded) — not while protection stays off. Admin may have intentionally disabled it, or an attacker with admin access has weakened defences.

elements.name: analytic7
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: IPS signature count dropped >50%

options.text: The IPS signature ruleset count has dropped by more than half compared to its 7-day max. The IPS threat-intel feed may be broken or rolling back — investigate to ensure threat detection remains effective.

elements.name: analytic8
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: ISP Downtime

options.text: Detects when the ISP connection experiences downtime. Any downtime impacts business operations and should be investigated and documented for SLA purposes.

elements.name: analytic9
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: ISP High Latency

options.text: Detects when ISP latency exceeds acceptable thresholds. High latency can impact business operations, VoIP quality, and may indicate ISP issues or network attacks.

elements.name: analytic10
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: ISP Packet Loss

options.text: Detects when packet loss is occurring on the WAN connection. Even small amounts of packet loss can significantly impact application performance, VoIP calls, and video conferencing.

elements.name: analytic11
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: ISP SLA Breach

options.text: Detects when ISP uptime falls below the SLA threshold. This alert helps track SLA compliance and can be used for ISP accountability and contract negotiations.

elements.name: analytic12
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Multiple Devices Offline

options.text: Detects when multiple UniFi devices go offline simultaneously. This typically indicates a network-wide issue such as power outage, upstream connectivity failure, or infrastructure problem requiring im

elements.name: analytic13
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: New Device Adopted

options.text: Detects when a new device is adopted into the UniFi network. While often legitimate, unexpected device adoptions could indicate unauthorized hardware being added to your network.

elements.name: analytic14
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: New WAN2 (secondary) issue recorded

options.text: A NEW issue appeared on the secondary WAN (`wans.WAN2.wanIssues[].index`). On multi-WAN sites this is the failover link — its downtime/latency matters even when WAN is healthy. Fires only on new issue indexes, not while existing issues persist.

elements.name: analytic15
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: New WAN issue index recorded

options.text: UniFi recorded a NEW WAN-side issue (new `wans.WAN.wanIssues[].index`) not seen in the previous poll. Fires once per new issue index — not repeatedly while a persistent issue is unresolved. May indicate ISP problems, hardware fault, or active DoS pressure on the gateway.

elements.name: analytic16
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: New critical notifications appeared

options.text: UniFi critical-notification count INCREASED since the previous poll, meaning at least one new critical-severity alarm appeared. Fires only on the increase, not while existing criticals persist unacknowledged.

elements.name: analytic17
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Pending firmware updates outstanding for 7d+

options.text: A site has had pending firmware updates continuously for the last 7 days. Indicates patch debt — schedule the updates.

elements.name: analytic18
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Site Health Critical

options.text: Detects when a site has multiple offline devices, indicating a significant site-level issue such as power outage, network failure, or infrastructure problem requiring immediate attention.

elements.name: analytic19
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: System log shipping disabled

options.text: A UniFi host transitioned `reportedState.cloudSystemLogState` from `enabled` to `disabled`. Cloud log shipping going dark is a defense-evasion signal — an attacker with admin access may be silencing audit trails.

elements.name: analytic20
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: WAN uptime below 99%

options.text: Site WAN uptime is below 99% — SLA-grade availability problem. Combine with ISP Downtime rule for root-cause attribution.

elements.name: analytic21
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: WiFi quality degraded (high TX retry)

options.text: Site-wide WiFi TX retry rate exceeded 5% — indicates RF interference, channel congestion, or undersized AP coverage. Could also signal active wireless attack (deauth flooding).
This solution installs the following hunting queries. After installing the solution, run these hunting queries to hunt for threats in Manage solution view.

elements.name: huntingquery1
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Console group membership churn

options.text: Tracks members of `userData.consoleGroupMembers` over time. Additions indicate new admin access; removals indicate revoked access. Both warrant review — additions are an account-creation signal, removals can be a sign of attacker cleanup. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_Cloud_Hosts_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery2
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Devices flapping online/offline

options.text: Devices that transitioned between online and offline more than 4 times in 24 hours. Flapping can indicate intermittent connectivity, hardware failure, environmental issues, or active interference targeting the device. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery3
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Firmware drift hotspots

options.text: Devices behind on firmware updates — ranked by product line and model. Identifies models where firmware drift is concentrated, indicating either a vendor-side delay or operational neglect. Each unpatched device is a CVE attack surface. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery4
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Firmware version diversity within a model

options.text: Models running multiple distinct firmware versions across the estate. Indicates inconsistent patch management — some units are forgotten or excluded from update windows. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery5
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Long-tail ISP latency hotspots (P95)

options.text: Sites where the P95 ISP latency is significantly above the median over the last 7 days. Helps identify sites suffering occasional but severe latency spikes that average-based monitoring misses. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_Cloud_ISPMetrics_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery6
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Devices adopted outside business hours

options.text: New devices adopted into a UniFi site outside business hours (10pm - 6am local time, or weekends). Legitimate admin work usually happens in hours; unattended adoption can be unauthorised hardware joining the network. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_Cloud_Devices_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery7
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: Sites with persistent WAN issues

options.text: Sites that have recorded WAN issues in the majority of polls over the last 24 hours. Indicates chronic ISP problems versus transient blips — escalate to provider. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery8
elements.label: UniFi Cloud: WAN external IP geographic deviation

options.text: Sites where the WAN external IP changed ASN or ISP within the last 30 days. Routine DHCP renewal stays within the same ISP; an ASN/ISP change suggests provider switch, BGP hijack, or routing anomaly that warrants verification. This hunting query depends on UniFiSiteManagerConnectorDefinition data connector (Unifi_Cloud_Sites_CL Parser or Table)
